implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Footer added directly to src/app/page.tsx with copyright text and current year, satisfying both acceptance criteria. Change is minimal and well-scoped. The implementation log file is a stub (all sections empty), which is a minor quality miss but not a functional regression.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria met: footer appears at bottom of page, contains current year and 'BrewBoard' copyright text. Spec called for one-line addition to page.tsx — done.

correctness.notes: Valid JSX, correct use of new Date().getFullYear(), proper Tailwind classes consistent with stone-* palette likely used elsewhere. No type or integration issues.

minimality.notes: 3-line addition to page.tsx, no refactors, no extra files beyond the log stub. Exactly the scope requested.

code_quality.notes: Footer markup is clean and readable. However, the implementation log was created with all section headers empty — it's a placeholder rather than an actual log, which weakens the artifact even though spec said no docs.

risk.notes: Semantic <footer> element is good for a11y. Note: spec said 'every page' but footer was only added to page.tsx (home), not to a layout — could be a missed scope on multi-page sites, though spec's technical approach explicitly named page.tsx so this is acceptable.
